name: Portland to Houston LTL
hero-headline: Portland, OR to Houston, TX LTL Freight
subheadline: 2,164 miles. 5-7 business day transit. 7 carriers active on this lane.
body-content:
Lane Overview: Portland to Houston

The Portland to Houston LTL lane covers approximately 2,164 miles between the Pacific Northwest and South Central regions. Standard transit runs 5-7 business days depending on carrier routing and terminal schedules. Common equipment on this corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Cross-dock facilities at Portland Terminal, Pacific NW Hub, Bay Area Hub support consolidation and transit optimization. Steady volumes with produce season lift Jun-Sep. Winter weather delays possible.

How WARP Operates the Portland to Houston Lane

WARP covers the Portland to Houston corridor with 7 vetted carriers providing LTL capacity.

  • Service capabilities on this lane include: Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery; Estimated 5-7 business day transit window; Pallet-level tracking with delivery appointment scheduling.
  • Shipments receive pallet-level tracking from pickup through delivery with appointment scheduling and delivery confirmation.
  • Nearby cross-dock operations at Portland Terminal and Pacific NW Hub enable consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur.
  • Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change.
  • WARP's operations team manages carrier coordination, rescheduling, and resolution without requiring shipper follow-up.

Operating Details

Key operational considerations for this lane:

  • Estimated lane distance: 2,164 miles (Portland to Houston)
  • Transit window: 5-7 business days for standard LTL service
  • Available equipment: Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled)
  • Active carriers on this lane: 7
  • Nearest cross-dock facilities: Portland Terminal, Pacific NW Hub, Bay Area Hub, Houston Terminal, Dallas Mega Hub
  • Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery milestones
  • Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status changes with automated escalation
  • Delivery appointment scheduling and confirmation managed by WARP operations

Pricing & Rate Factors

Estimated LTL rates on the Portland to Houston lane range from $9,515 to $12,687, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand.

  • Key cost drivers include NMFC freight classification, shipment density, and whether accessorials such as liftgate, inside delivery, or limited-access pickup apply.
  • At 2,164 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $5.
  • 13/mile).
  • Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices.
  • These are modeled estimates.

Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

Validate This Lane

Validate this lane with a controlled pilot: Portland to Houston LTL (2,164-mile corridor; 5–7 day transit window; 7 vetted carriers active; cross-dock access at Portland Terminal and Pacific NW Hub). Track quote response time, transit predictability, and exception trends before scaling volume. Start with a single shipment to benchmark WARP against your current process.

faq-schema:
How much does LTL freight from Portland, OR to Houston, TX cost?

Estimated LTL rates range from $9,515 to $12,687 based on freight class, weight, and pallet count. Actual rates depend on shipment specifics and current market conditions. Get an instant quote for real-time pricing.

What is the transit time for LTL shipments from Portland to Houston?

Standard LTL transit on this 2,164-mile lane is 5-7 business days. Actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Portland to Houston lane?

WARP maintains a vetted network of approximately 7 active carriers on this corridor. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and performance history.

Can I track my LTL shipment from Portland to Houston in real time?

Yes. Every shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly.

What equipment is available for LTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Portland to Houston corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.
